ignore-generated-files.label: Keep Generated Files Out of History
ignore-generated-files.summary: Add exact ignore rules and remove generated output from future tracked snapshots.

sketch2.title: Tracked Files Need an Extra Step

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.title: Write the Project Ignore Rules
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.prompt: Create `community-project/.gitignore`. Starting on line 1, enter exactly `generated/` and `*.log`, one rule per line, then save. Create `debug.log` and `generated/preview.txt`, and inspect status with ignored paths included. Do not stage anything yet.
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.hint: The ignore file contains two exact lines; the new log and preview should appear as ignored.

help.concept: .gitignore describes untracked paths Git should normally leave out of status and staging.
help.hint_1: Use exactly generated/ and *.log on separate lines.
help.hint_2: The generated preview and log should appear as ignored in the requested status view.

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.starterCode:
# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.solutionCode:
touch debug.log
touch generated/preview.txt
git status --short --ignored
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.instructions: Create `community-project/.gitignore`. Starting on line 1, enter exactly `generated/` and `*.log`, one rule per line, then save. Create `debug.log` and `generated/preview.txt`, and inspect status with ignored paths included. Do not stage anything yet.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- community-project
mkdir -p -- community-project
git -C community-project init -q -b main
git -C community-project config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner'
git -C community-project config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community schedule

Saturday cleanup: 10:00 AM
' > community-project/schedule.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community contacts

Help desk: 555-0100
' > community-project/contacts.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Old planning notes

Spring flyer draft.
' > community-project/old-notes.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project/generated
printf %s 'Generated summary

Created automatically from project notes.
' > community-project/generated/summary.txt
git -C community-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-project commit -q -m 'Start community project'
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community schedule

Saturday cleanup: 9:30 AM
' > community-project/schedule.txt
git -C community-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z git -C community-project commit -q -m 'Update community schedule'
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community contacts

Help desk: 555-0130
' > community-project/contacts.txt
git -C community-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z git -C community-project commit -q -m 'Update community contacts'
rm -f -- community-project/schedule.txt
rm -f -- community-project/old-notes.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community schedule

Saturday cleanup: 9:30 AM
' > community-project/events.txt
git -C community-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-04T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-04T12:00:00Z git -C community-project commit -q -m 'Organize community project files'

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch2.title: Stop Tracking the Generated Summary
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch2.prompt: `generated/summary.txt` was committed before the ignore rule existed. Stage `.gitignore`, then remove `generated/summary.txt` from Git tracking while keeping the file in the working directory.
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch2.hint: Apply the ignore policy and remove only the tracked relationship, not the local file.

help.concept: Ignore rules do not automatically untrack a file already in history.
help.hint_1: The file should still exist after the command.

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch2.instructions: `generated/summary.txt` was committed before the ignore rule existed. Stage `.gitignore`, then remove `generated/summary.txt` from Git tracking while keeping the file in the working directory.

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.title: Record the Ignore Policy
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.prompt: The ignore rules and the tracked-file removal are staged. Record them with the message `Ignore generated output`, then confirm that normal short status is clean.
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.hint: Commit the policy and verify that ignored local files no longer create normal status noise.

help.concept: The commit records the policy and removes generated output from future tracked snapshots.
help.hint_2: Ignored files may remain on disk while short status stays clean.

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.solutionCode:
git commit -m "Ignore generated output"
git status --short
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.instructions: The ignore rules and the tracked-file removal are staged. Record them with the message `Ignore generated output`, then confirm that normal short status is clean.

ignore-generated-files-single-choice-0.title: Purpose of .gitignore
ignore-generated-files-single-choice-0.prompt: What is the primary purpose of a `.gitignore` file in a Git repository?
ignore-generated-files-single-choice-0.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Write narrow ignore rules, prove which rule matches, and separate “ignored” from “already tracked.”
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: To track changes in all files
options.b: To ignore specific files and directories
options.c: To delete files from the repository
options.d: To rename files in the repository

ignore-generated-files-single-choice-1.title: Effect of Adding to .gitignore
ignore-generated-files-single-choice-1.prompt: What happens when you add a file to `.gitignore` that has already been committed?
ignore-generated-files-single-choice-1.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Write narrow ignore rules, prove which rule matches, and separate “ignored” from “already tracked.”
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: The file is automatically deleted from the repository
options.b: The file is untracked and removed from history
options.c: The file remains tracked and in history
options.d: The file is renamed in the repository

ignore-generated-files-multi-choice-0.title: Useful Ignore Patterns
ignore-generated-files-multi-choice-0.prompt: Which patterns match generated or local-only files used in this course? Select all that apply.
ignore-generated-files-multi-choice-0.hint: Judge each option independently; choose only the statements that stay true in this exact repository state.

help.concept: Write narrow ignore rules, prove which rule matches, and separate “ignored” from “already tracked.”
help.hint_1: A partly true option is still wrong if it adds an effect Git does not guarantee.
help.hint_2: Use the layer named in the prompt—working tree, staging area, or history—to test each choice.

ignore-generated-files-drag-reorder-0.title: Stop Tracking a Local-Only File
ignore-generated-files-drag-reorder-0.prompt: Arrange the safe steps for a file that is already tracked but should remain only on each developer’s machine.
ignore-generated-files-drag-reorder-0.hint: Start with the state that must exist before the next action can make sense.

help.concept: Write narrow ignore rules, prove which rule matches, and separate “ignored” from “already tracked.”
help.hint_1: Track how the project moves from working files to a proposed snapshot and then to history.
help.hint_2: Read the ordered steps as one continuous repository story.

tokens.t2: Remove the path from Git’s index while keeping the working copy

ignore-generated-files-sketch0.title: Ignore Rules Describe Re-creatable Files
ignore-generated-files-sketch0.bodyMarkdown: Generated summaries, previews, and logs can be recreated. `.gitignore` keeps matching untracked paths out of normal staging and status while the project’s authored text files remain visible.

ignore-generated-files-sketch2.title: Tracked Files Need an Extra Step
ignore-generated-files-sketch2.bodyMarkdown: A new ignore rule does not remove a file that Git already tracks. Removing it only from the index preserves the local working copy while excluding it from future snapshots.

ignore-generated-files-sketch3.title: The Policy Is Project Source
ignore-generated-files-sketch3.bodyMarkdown: Commit `.gitignore` with the tracked-file cleanup. Generated text remains on disk and ignored while the tracked repository ends clean.
